output:
CVE-2009-10002 is a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability identified in the dpup fittr-flickr's EXIF Preview Handler, specifically affecting the processing in the file fittr-flickr/features/easy-exif.js. This vulnerability, classified under CWE-79, arises because the software fails to properly neutralize user-controllable input before it is placed in an output used as a web page served to other users.

The severity of this vulnerability is rated as medium, indicating a significant potential impact if exploited. The attack can be initiated remotely, posing a risk to data integrity and privacy.

Affected software includes any version of fittr_flickr used with Chrome prior to 2009-11-03. Users are recommended to apply the patch identified by 08875dd8a2e5d0d16568bb0d67cb4328062fccde to mitigate the issue effectively. Implementing documented mitigation techniques such as M1048 and M1037 can also help manage the risk associated with this vulnerability.

This CVE is associated with the MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1190, reflecting its relevance to exploitation processes involving injection techniques.
